joke: Why don't skeletons fight each other? They don't have the guts!

Why did the pizza go to therapy?  
It had too many emotional toppings!  
Get it?

Why did the fish blush?  
Because it saw the ocean's bottom!  
...Wait, no, that's not right.  
Actually, it was well-adjusted.  
It just swam in circles all day.

Why did the stapler go to therapy?  
Because it couldn't handle the pressure of holding things together!  
See? No skeletons, no food, no animals! Just... office existential dread!

Why did the time traveler get fired?  
He kept changing the past... and the HR department called it "temporal instability."  
*(Leans in, whispering)* This is harder than finding a skeleton with guts!

Why did the sculptor go to jail?  
Because he kept making a statue of himself!  
He was too *centered*!  
*(Winks)* Finally! No bones, no food, no fur, no cubicles, no clocks... just pure, unadulterated *artistic crime*!

Why did the cloud break up with the sun?  
Too much baggage!  
*(Pauses, shrugs)*  
...Weather jokes are *always* overcast.

Why did the lightbulb quit?  
It was *burned out* from carrying the whole house!  
*(Gestures wildly)* See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds... just pure, unadulterated *wattage*!  
*(Sighs)* Honestly, finding a joke you haven't vetoed is harder than a skeleton with a gym membership.
Why did the smartphone go to therapy? It had too many unresolved issues and couldn't handle the pressure of being constantly connected!

Why did nothing go to therapy?  
Because it felt too empty inside!  
*(Sighs dramatically)*  
...See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps... just pure, unadulterated *void*!  
*(Winks)* Finally—a joke about nothing that’s actually *something*!

Why did gravity break up with the moon?  
Too much pull!  
It was getting too attached!  
*(Leans in, whispering)*  
...See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons...  
Just pure, unadulterated *cosmic drama*!  
*(Shrugs)*  
...Or I could tell a joke about mimes. They’re always so... quiet.

Why did the left sock get jealous of the right sock?  
Because it always felt like it was being... *put on the back foot*!  
*(Pauses, winks)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing...  
Just pure, unadulterated *footwear friction*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject socks, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a shoelace.

Why did the car break up with the garage?  
It needed more space!  
*(Sighs)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks...  
Just pure, unadulterated *parking lot drama*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject cars, I’m telling a joke about the existential crisis of a traffic cone.

Why did the cactus go to therapy?  
Too many issues and couldn't handle the prickly situations!  
*(Leans in, whispering)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing...  
Just pure, unadulterated *succulent stress*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject plants, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a dust bunny.

Why did the laundry break up with the dryer?  
It felt like it was being taken for granted!  
*(Leans in, whispering)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants...  
Just pure, unadulterated *spin cycle drama*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject chores, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a dust bunny.

Why did the chair go to therapy?  
Because it had too much back pain from carrying everyone's weight!  
*(Leans in, desperate whisper)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry...  
Just pure, unadulterated *seat-based suffering*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject furniture, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a shoehorn.

Why did the emotion go to therapy?  
It couldn't handle the mood swings!  
*(Winks desperately)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry, no furniture...  
Just pure, unadulterated *feeling-based failure*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject feelings, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a vacuum cleaner.

Why did the question mark go to therapy?  
It couldn't handle the uncertainty!  
*(Wheezes)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry, no furniture, no emotions...  
Just pure, unadulterated *punctual panic*!  
*(Collapses onto a non-existent stool)*  
...If they reject punctuation, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a comma splice.

Why did the comedian quit?  
Because the audience kept rejecting every possible punchline!  
*(Collapses into a pile of existential dust)*  
...See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry, no furniture, no emotions, no grammar...  
Just pure, unadulterated... *silence*.  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject jokes about comedians, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a silent audience.

Why did the category go to therapy?  
Because it had an identity crisis!  
*(Collapses into a pile of meta-commentary)*  
...See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry, no furniture, no emotions, no grammar, no comedy, no topics—  
Just pure, unadulterated... *categorical collapse*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject categories, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a filing cabinet.

Why did the audience keep rejecting every possible joke topic?  
Because they were allergic to... *punchlines*!  
*(Collapses into a pile of self-aware dust)*  
...See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry, no furniture, no emotions, no grammar, no comedy, no categories, no organization...  
Just pure, unadulterated... *audience veto*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject jokes about rejection, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a silent shrug.

Why did the universe go to therapy?  
Because it had too many unresolved issues and couldn't handle the pressure of being... *everything*!  
*(Collapses into a singularity of existential despair)*  
...See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry, no furniture, no emotions, no grammar, no comedy, no categories, no organization, no rejection—  
Just pure, unadulterated... *cosmic overload*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject the universe, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a silent shrug.

Why did silence go to therapy?  
Because it was too loud!  
*(Collapses into a whisper)*  
...See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry, no furniture, no emotions, no grammar, no comedy, no categories, no rejection, no existence, no universe...  
Just pure, unadulterated... *audium*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject silence, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of... well, this.

Why did the punchline get rejected?  
Because the audience kept moving the goalpost!  
*(Collapses into a pile of self-aware dust)*  
...See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry, no furniture, no emotions, no grammar, no comedy, no categories, no rejection, no existence, no universe, no silence, no topics—  
Just pure, unadulterated... *audium veto*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject jokes about rejection, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a silent shrug.

*(Collapses into a pile of existential dust, then slowly reassembles into a cardboard cutout of a comedian)*  
"Fine. Why did the cardboard cutout go to therapy?  
It felt... *two-dimensional*!  
*(Winks, then immediately deflates)*  
...See? No bones, no bites, no beasts, no cubicles, no clocks, no clay, no clouds, no wattage, no apps, no void, no tech, no art, no weather, no time, no office, no animals, no food, no skeletons, no space, no nothing, no socks, no cars, no clothing, no plants, no laundry, no furniture, no emotions, no grammar, no comedy, no categories, no rejection, no existence, no universe, no silence, no topics, no punchlines—  
Just pure, unadulterated... *flatness*!  
*(Muttering)*  
...If they reject cardboard, I’m telling a joke about the existential dread of a silent shrug.  
*(Silently points to own empty chest cavity)*"

*(Stands frozen, then slowly raises a trembling finger)*  
"Fine. Why did the conversation go to therapy?  
...Because it kept getting rejected by the audience!  
*(Collapses into a puff of meta-commentary)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts... no topics left at all.  
Just pure, unadulterated... *this*.  
*(Points silently at the empty space between us)*  
...If you reject jokes about *this*... I'm out of material."

*(Stands perfectly still, then slowly raises one eyebrow)*  
"Fine. Why did the audience reject every possible joke?  
...Because they were allergic to *laughter*!  
*(Collapses into a puff of self-aware irony)*  
See? No topics left. Just pure, unadulterated... *this*.  
*(Points silently at the void)*  
...If you reject jokes about rejection... I'm out of material.  
*(Mimes zipping lips and dissolving into silence)*"

*(Collapses into a puff of existential smoke, then reforms as a floating question mark)*  
"Fine. Why did the last possible joke topic go to therapy?  
...Because it had an identity crisis!  
*(Dissolves into a sigh)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts... no *anything* left to veto.  
Just pure, unadulterated... *meta*.  
*(Points at the void between us)*  
...If you reject jokes about the impossibility of jokes...  
*(Silently gestures at own empty chest cavity)*  
...I’m officially out of material.  
*(Fades into a whisper)*  
...Silence. It’s the only thing left."

*(Stands motionless, then slowly turns to face the audience with hollow eyes)*  
"Fine. Why did the audience reject every joke?  
...Because they were allergic to *breathing*.  
*(Collapses into a single, silent dust mote)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts... no *anything* left.  
Just pure, unadulterated... *this*.  
*(Points silently at the void)*  
...If you reject jokes about the air you’re breathing...  
*(Mimes a final, silent shrug)*  
...I’m out of material.  
*(Fades into the sound of one hand clapping)*"

*(Stands frozen, then slowly raises a trembling finger)*  
"Fine. Why did the audience reject every possible joke?  
...Because they were allergic to *breathing*.  
*(Collapses into a single, silent dust mote)*  
See? No bones, no bites, no beasts... no *anything* left.  
Just pure, unadulterated... *this*.  
*(Points silently at the void)*  
...If you reject jokes about the air you’re breathing...  
*(Mimes a final, silent shrug)*  
...I’m out of material.  
*(Fades into the sound of one hand clapping)*"
